news.headline: GM will cease robotaxi operations previously supported by CEO Barra.
Notation Labs secured $2 million credit facility to boost production of Qwel™ for lead detection, smart home tech.
news.summary: A U.S.-based facility in Scottsdale, Arizona received funding totaling $2 million to enhance production capabilities and introduce cutting-edge lead detection and smart home technology to the market. Notation Labs Inc. confirmed the conclusion of the credit facility on December 10, 2024, signaling a significant step towards expanding operations and innovation in the industry.

news.headline: Argentina’s Milei dismisses tax agency head after imposing tax on streamers, appoints new leader.
news.summary: The country's President, Javier Milei, has recently made changes to the leadership of the revenue agency. This decision comes after the implementation of a new tax system aimed at digital creators. The revamped leadership is expected to oversee the enforcement and administration of this taxation. The move signifies a shift in the government's approach towards regulating the digital economy and ensuring compliance. The exact number of officials replaced has not been disclosed, but it is a significant development in the country's fiscal policies under Milei's administration.

news.headline: Neal Gerber Eisenberg to move to Chicago landmark The Bell in 2025, occupying floors 27-29, from current office.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, Neal Gerber Eisenberg (NGE) revealed their intention to relocate to The Bell at 225 West Randolph Street, Chicago, in May 2025. This move marks a shift from their present location at Two North LaSalle Street. The newly renovated site, formerly known as the Illinois Bell Building and recognized as a Chicago landmark in 2021, holds historical significance since its establishment in 1966. Upon relocation, NGE will be situated on floors 27 to 29 of the iconic tower. The transition promises modern office spaces for attorneys and staff to operate efficiently.

news.headline: Luke Johnson Scholarship in Scottsdale offers $1,000 one-time award to support entrepreneurial innovators.
news.summary: In Scottsdale, Arizona on December 10, 2024, the Luke Johnson Scholarship for Entrepreneurs has revealed its mission to aid upcoming entrepreneurial innovators with a one-time award of $1,000. Luke Johnson, a respected entrepreneur from Scottsdale, established this scholarship with the purpose of supporting the future generation of business pioneers.

news.headline: Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. reported $166.8 billion AUM on November 30, 2024, with Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds totaling $80.5 billion, and separate accounts accounting for $86.3 billion. Artisan Funds made distributions with $314 million not reinvested.
news.summary: Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. announced that its preliminary assets under management as of November 30, 2024, amounted to $166.8 billion. Among this total, Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds represented $80.5 billion while separate accounts and other assets under management accounted for $86.3 billion. During November, specific Artisan Funds distributed their annual income and capital gains. The month-end AUM for November reflects the influence of roughly $314 million from Artisan Funds distributions that were not reinvested.

news.headline: IFF names Michael DeVeau as CFO effective Jan. 1, 2025, succeeding Glenn Richter who will retire by end of 2024.
news.summary: In New York, IFF (NYSE: IFF) has appointed Michael DeVeau as its new Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, starting on Jan. 1, 2025. DeVeau, who currently serves as Senior Vice President of Corporate Finance and Investor Relations, will take over from Glenn Richter, the current CFO and Business Transformation Officer, upon his planned retirement at the end of 2024. The company expressed their satisfaction with DeVeau's promotion, describing him as the ideal candidate for the role.

Landstar System, Inc. is listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol LSTR and is known for its technology-driven, asset-light transportation management services, focusing on safe and specialized logistics solutions.

Man who stole funds for sick kids & vets sentenced to 10 years for keeping 80% of donations meant for charities.
news.summary: In September, Richard Zeitlin, a prominent figure in the telemarketing industry, admitted to committing fraud by withholding 80% of the donations collected for charitable purposes.

Dan Schwartz appointed as NABH Director of Quality &Addiction Services, boasting diverse federal agency experiences.

news.headline: ALS specialists diagnose patients twice as fast as general neurologists. New thinkALS™ tool helps providers identify and refer patients quicker. ARLINGTON, Va., Dec. 10, 2024.
news.summary: An innovative tool called thinkALS™ has been introduced to assist providers in identifying and referring patients more efficiently. Research indicates that individuals seen in general neurology clinics may experience delays in receiving an ALS diagnosis, taking up to twice as long compared to those seen in specialist clinics. This groundbreaking tool aims to bridge this gap and streamline the process of identifying and referring ALS patients promptly. The development of thinkALS™ could potentially significantly reduce the time taken for patients to receive a proper diagnosis and access necessary care. The tool's implementation is expected to contribute to expedited referrals, ultimately improving the overall management and outcomes for individuals affected by ALS. The release of thinkALS™ promises to revolutionize the diagnostic pathway for ALS, potentially leading to more timely interventions and support for patients.

news.headline: Caterpillar Inc.'s board of directors maintains $1.41 quarterly dividend per share, payable on Feb. 20, 2025.
news.summary: In Irving, Texas, on December 10, 2024, the Board of Directors of Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) decided to keep the quarterly dividend unchanged at $1.41 per share of common stock. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on February 20, 2025, to shareholders of record at the end of business hours.

news.headline: BODi launches "Belle Vitale" by Autumn Calabrese, a 12-week program for women's hormone health. NYSE: BODI.
news.summary: The Beachbody Company, Inc. (NYSE: BODI) based in El Segundo, California, has introduced a new program called "Belle Vitale," developed by Autumn Calabrese, a fitness and nutrition expert. The program aims to assist women in dealing with problems related to hormone imbalances at any age. Autumn Calabrese is an IIN Certified Hormone Health Coach. The initiative takes a holistic approach to address issues and symptoms arising from hormonal imbalances as per a Northwell Health article.

news.headline: Talen Energy increases term loan B financing from $600 million to $850 million, repurchases common stock.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, Talen Energy Corporation, traded as TLN on NASDAQ, revealed an increase in its incremental Term Loan B credit facility from $600 million to $850 million. This initiative, referred to as the "Upsize," signifies a significant expansion in the financial resources available to the Company.

news.headline: Nasdaq reports end-of-month short interest in 3,065 stocks at 11.8B shares as of Nov 29, 2024, up from 11.97B on Nov 15.
news.summary: In New York on December 10, 2024, it was reported that short interest in 3,065 Nasdaq Global MarketSM securities reached 11,857,580,215 shares at the end of the settlement date of November 29, 2024. This marked a decrease from 11,973,515,318 shares in 3,070 Global Market issues recorded for the previous settlement date of November 15, 2024. The short interest calculated at the end of November signifies 2.36 days, showing an increase from the 2.25 days reported during the prior reporting period.

news.headline: Globalink Investment Inc. extends business combination deadline to Jan 9, 2025, with $60,000 deposited, 18th extension since IPO.
news.summary: In New York City on December 10, 2024, Globalink Investment Inc. (Nasdaq: GLLI, GLLIW, GLLIR, GLLIU) disclosed that it has placed $60,000 (referred to as the “Extension Payment”) into its Trust Account held by Continental Stock Transfer and Trust Company. This deposit effectively extends the deadline for its initial business combination from December 9, 2024, to January 9, 2025. This move marks the eighteenth extension since the completion of the Company’s initial public offering on December 9, 2021. Additionally, the Company's governing documents allow for up to six extensions, and this is the first one utilized within that limit.

news.headline: Apollomics Inc. regains Nasdaq compliance by maintaining minimum bid price of $1.00/share, ensuring listing as APLM.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, Apollomics Inc. (Nasdaq: APLM), a Foster City-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing treatments for challenging cancers, disclosed that it had received a notification from The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC. The notification confirmed that the company complied with the rule necessitating a minimum closing bid price of $1.00 per share as outlined in Nasdaq Rule 5550(a)(2). Consequently, Nasdaq decided to maintain the listing of the Company’s Class A ordinary shares ("Class A Ordinary Shares") on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker symbol "APLM."

news.headline: Orchid Island Capital declares December dividend of $0.12 per share, paid on Jan 30, 2025, record date Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: Orchid Island Capital, Inc. disclosed today in Vero Beach, Florida, that its Board of Directors approved a monthly cash dividend for December 2024. Each shareholder of record as of December 31, 2024, will receive $0.12 per share, with the payment scheduled for January 30, 2025. The ex-dividend date is also December 31, 2024. The next common stock dividend declaration is set to be announced on January 8, 2025. The Company's ticker on the New York Stock Exchange is ORC.

news.headline: Yellen discussed with Bessent post nomination; shared fiscal regrets.
ACM Research enhances ALD portfolio with furnace tools for 300mm semiconductor production.
news.summary: State-of-the-art Thermal and Plasma-Enhanced Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD) furnace tools have been approved for use in mass production of 300mm semiconductor wafers. These tools are equipped to handle high-volume manufacturing, reflecting the industry's shift towards larger wafer sizes to accommodate increased production demands. The qualification of these tools signifies a major advancement in semiconductor manufacturing technology, with the potential to significantly improve efficiency and output.

news.headline: Domo partners with Richey May to offer data solutions to mortgage brokers, benefiting over 80 clients.

Over 80 mortgage bankers within Richey May's clientele have already utilized Domo's platform to access valuable insights to enhance their operations. With over four decades of experience in the mortgage sector, Richey May is known for its comprehensive advisory and technological services.

news.headline: Critical Metals Corp appoints Steven R Parkes as new CFO, replacing Melissa Chapman. Carolyn Trabuco steps down as Director.

Additionally, Ms. Carolyn Trabuco has resigned from her position as an Independent Director of the Board.
